stem: A industrial safety safety system uses 3 parallel sensors for redundancy. The hazard detected requires at least one sensor to function. sensor 1 has a reliability between 68% and 86% (uncertain due to limited operational data). sensor 2 has a reliability of 95%. sensor 3 has a reliability of 76%. What is the overall probability that the hazard detected?

ground_truth.answer: P(hazard detected) ranges from 0.9962 to 0.9983. Point estimate: 0.9972.

solution_steps: P(system works) = 1 - P(all 3 sensors fail simultaneously).
At lower bound: P(all fail) = 0.32 × 0.05 × 0.24 = 0.0038. P(works) = 0.9962.
At upper bound: P(all fail) = 0.14 × 0.05 × 0.24 = 0.0017. P(works) = 0.9983.
common_failure_modes: Multiplying individual reliabilities instead of computing 1 - P(all fail)
Using a single reliability for the uncertain component without propagating uncertainty
Treating the system as a series (all must work) rather than parallel (any one suffices)
